No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to ending hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) providing professional legal knowledge and problem-solving skills for international business (Target 4.4). It also touches on sustainability issues (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly covers labor law issues and risks (Target 8.8) and aims to establish sound business order for international business operations, supporting economic growth and decent job creation (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course examines legal frameworks for international business and cross-border M&A (Week 10), which supports inclusive and sustainable industrialization (Target 9.2) and access to financial services (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers WTO principles (Target 10.a) and financial crime risks like anti-money laundering (Target 10.5), which contribute to fairer global economic systems and better regulation, thus reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's core competency includes understanding and identifying sustainability issues, offering a tangential link to encouraging companies to adopt sustainable practices (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's core focus is on legal frameworks, compliance, and establishing sound business order (Target 16.3). It explicitly covers anti-money laundering (Target 16.4) and anti-corruption (Target 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course covers WTO principles, promoting a rules-based trading system (Target 17.10). It also involves multi-stakeholder partnerships by inviting experts from academia, industry, and government (Target 17.17).
